title: git init and git clone
content:
### git init — Create a New Repository

Initializes a new Git repository in the current directory:

```bash
git init
```

To create a repo in a specific directory:

```bash
git init my-project
```

### git clone — Copy an Existing Repository

Downloads a remote repository to your local machine:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/user/repo.git
```

### Cloning Specific Branches

Clone only a particular branch (saves bandwidth):

```bash
git clone --branch main --single-branch https://github.com/user/repo.git
```

### Shallow Clone

Clone with limited history using `--depth`:

```bash
git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/user/repo.git
```

Useful for large repos where full history isn't needed (e.g., CI/CD).

### Bare Repositories

A bare repo has no working tree — only Git data. Used as a central hub:

```bash
git init --bare repo.git
```

Bare repos are standard for remote "origin" servers (GitHub, GitLab).
codeExample:
# Create new repo
git init my-app
cd my-app
echo "# My App" > README.md
git add README.md
git commit -m "initial commit"

# Clone existing repo
git clone https://github.com/user/existing-project.git

# Shallow clone
git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/user/large-repo.git

quiz.question: Which flag limits clone history to a specific depth?
What is a bare repository used for?
